report_title: Plugin and MCP Initialization Path Audit — opencode Sidecar Server
summary: Plugin layer initializes internal plugins (9 built-ins) eagerly during bootstrap, then loads external plugins from config with per-plugin error isolation. MCP servers connect concurrently via layer init with silent error suppression. Neither plugin nor MCP failures crash the process. Native binary loading is on-demand (spawn model), not at startup.

init_phases.description: Plugin service initialization — loads internal providers, then external plugins from config

defaultLayer_composition.code: export const defaultLayer = layer.pipe(Layer.provide(Bus.layer), Layer.provide(Config.defaultLayer), Layer.provide(RuntimeFlags.defaultLayer))

Service_class.code: export class Service extends Context.Service<Service, Interface>()("@opencode/Plugin") {}

internalPlugins_list.code: function internalPlugins(flags: RuntimeFlags.Info): PluginInstance[] { return [ (input) => CodexAuthPlugin(input, { experimentalWebSockets: ... }), CopilotAuthPlugin, GitlabAuthPlugin, PoeAuthPlugin, CloudflareWorkersAuthPlugin, CloudflareAIGatewayAuthPlugin, AzureAuthPlugin, DigitalOceanAuthPlugin, XaiAuthPlugin ] }

internal_plugin_load_loop.code: Effect.forEach(internalPlugins(flags), (plugin) => Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => plugin(input), catch: (err) => { log.error("failed to load internal plugin", { name: plugin.name, error: err }) } }).pipe(Effect.option) — if success, add to hooks[]; if failure, log error and skip

external_plugin_load.code: const plugins = flags.pure ? [] : (cfg.plugin_origins ?? []); if (plugins.length) yield* config.waitForDependencies(); const loaded = yield* Effect.promise(() => PluginLoader.loadExternal({ items: plugins, kind: "server", report: { start, missing, error } }))

applyPlugin.code: async function applyPlugin(load: PluginLoader.Loaded, input: PluginInput, hooks: Hooks[]): Promise<string> — calls plugin factory with PluginInput; on error, publishPluginError is called

publishPluginError.code: function publishPluginError(message: string) { bridge.fork(bus.publish(Session.Event.Error, { error: new NamedError.Unknown({ message }).toObject() })) }

config_hook_error.code: Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => Promise.resolve((hook as any).config?.(configForPlugin)), catch: (err) => { log.error("plugin config hook failed", { pluginId: ... , error: err }) } }).pipe(Effect.ignore)

dispose_hook_error.code: Effect.addFinalizer(() => Effect.forEach(hooks, (hook) => Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => Promise.resolve(hook.dispose?.()), catch: (error) => { log.error("plugin dispose hook failed", { error }) } }).pipe(Effect.ignore), { discard: true }))

quarantine_logic.code: entry.crashCount++ if crashCount >= MAX_CRASHES_BEFORE_QUARANTINE => entry.quarantined = true; log.warn("plugin auto-quarantined after repeated crashes")

unquarantine.code: yield* reg?.capabilityRegistry?.setCrashCount?.(pluginId, 0); yield* reg?.capabilityRegistry?.setQuarantined?.(pluginId, false); log.info("plugin manually un-quarantined")

health_report.code: const hr = yield* Effect.serviceOption(HealthRegistry); if (Option.isSome(hr)) { yield* hr.value.set("plugin", { status: HealthStatus.Healthy, updatedAt: Date.now() }) }

loadExternal.code: export async function loadExternal<R = Loaded>(input: Input<R>): Promise<R[]> — resolves and loads all configured plugins in parallel via Promise.all

resolve_stages.code: export async function resolve(plan, kind) — Stages: install (resolvePluginTarget), entry (createPluginEntry), compatibility (checkPluginCompatibility). Returns { ok: true, value: Resolved } or { ok: false, stage, error/value }

load_module.code: export async function load(row) — dynamic import(row.entry); returns { ok: true, value: Loaded } or { ok: false, error }

retry_logic.code: isRetryableResolveError: only "install" stage with "missing package.json or index file" message is retried; file-based plugins get one retry after dependency waiting

init_order.code: yield* plugin.init() // Comment: "Plugin can mutate config so it has to be initialized before anything else."

parallel_init.code: yield* Effect.forEach([reference, lsp, shareNext, format, file, fileWatcher, vcs, snapshot, project], (s) => s.init().pipe(Effect.catchCause((cause) => Effect.logWarning("init failed", { cause }))), { concurrency: "unbounded", discard: true })

error_handling.internal_plugin_failure: Caught by Effect.tryPromise catch block; logged at error level; plugin is skipped (Effect.option, Some vs None); process continues
error_handling.external_plugin_resolution_failure: Reported via report.error callback; logged; plugin is skipped from loaded list
error_handling.external_plugin_load_failure: Dynamic import failure is permanent (Bun caches failed module resolution); reported via report.error; plugin is skipped
error_handling.plugin_hook_failure: Caught by Effect.tryPromise catch; logged; hook result is ignored (Effect.ignore); process continues
error_handling.config_hook_failure: Caught by Effect.tryPromise catch; logged at error level; ignored (Effect.ignore)
error_handling.dispose_hook_failure: Caught by Effect.tryPromise catch; logged at error level; ignored (Effect.ignore)
error_handling.crash_quarantine: Plugin hook crashes tracked per pluginId; after MAX_CRASHES_BEFORE_QUARANTINE (3), plugin is auto-quarantined; persists to PluginHealthStore JSON; quarantined plugins have hooks skipped via dispatchGuard

init_phases.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server client initialization — connects to configured local/remote MCP servers

defaultLayer_composition.code: export const defaultLayer = layer.pipe(Layer.provide(McpAuth.layer), Layer.provide(Bus.layer), Layer.provide(Config.defaultLayer), Layer.provide(CrossSpawnSpawner.defaultLayer), Layer.provide(AppFileSystem.defaultLayer))

Service_class.code: export class Service extends Context.Service<Service, Interface>()("@opencode/MCP") {}

init_function.code: Effect.fn("MCP.state")(function* () { const cfg = yield* cfgSvc.get(); const config = cfg.mcp ?? {}; yield* Effect.forEach(Object.entries(config), ([key, mcp]) => Effect.gen(function* () { if (!isMcpConfigured(mcp)) { log.error("Ignoring MCP config entry without type", { key }); return } if (mcp.enabled === false) { s.status[key] = { status: "disabled" }; return } const result = yield* create(key, mcp).pipe(Effect.catch(() => Effect.void)); if (!result) return; s.status[key] = result.status; if (result.mcpClient) { s.clients[key] = result.mcpClient; s.defs[key] = result.defs!; watch(s, key, result.mcpClient, bridge, mcp.timeout) } }), { concurrency: "unbounded" })

connectTransport.code: const connectTransport = (transport: Transport, timeout: number) => Effect.acquireUseRelease(Effect.succeed(transport), (t) => Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => { const client = new Client(...); return withTimeout(client.connect(t), timeout).then(() => client) }, catch: (e) => (e instanceof Error ? e : new Error(String(e))) }), (t, exit) => (Exit.isFailure(exit) ? Effect.tryPromise(() => t.close()).pipe(Effect.ignore) : Effect.void))

connectLocal.code: const connectLocal = Effect.fn("MCP.connectLocal")(function* (key, mcp) { const [cmd, ...args] = mcp.command; const transport = new StdioClientTransport({ stderr: "pipe", command: cmd, args, cwd, env: { ...process.env, ...(cmd === "opencode" ? { BUN_BE_BUN: "1" } : {}), ...mcp.environment } }); transport.stderr?.on("data", (chunk) => { log.info(`mcp stderr: ${chunk.toString()}`, { key }) }); const connectTimeout = mcp.timeout ?? DEFAULT_TIMEOUT; return yield* connectTransport(transport, connectTimeout).pipe(Effect.map(...)) })

connectRemote.code: const connectRemote = Effect.fn("MCP.connectRemote")(function* (key, mcp) { tries SSE, then StreamableHTTP transports; OAuth support via McpOAuthProvider; on auth error, stops trying further transports; returns { client, status } })

create_function.code: const create = Effect.fn("MCP.create")(function* (key, mcp) { dispatches to connectLocal or connectRemote based on mcp.type; wraps in Effect.catch(() => Effect.void) in the caller

watch_tool_changes.code: function watch(s, name, client, bridge, timeout) — sets up ToolListChangedNotificationSchema handler; re-fetches tool defs on notification; publishes ToolsChanged bus event

cleanup_finalizer.code: Effect.addFinalizer(() => Effect.forEach(Object.values(s.clients), (client) => Effect.gen(function* () { const pid = client.transport instanceof StdioClientTransport ? client.transport.pid : null; if (typeof pid === "number") { const pids = yield* descendants(pid); for (const dpid of pids) { try { process.kill(dpid, "SIGTERM") } catch {} } } yield* Effect.tryPromise(() => client.close()).pipe(Effect.ignore) }), { concurrency: "unbounded" }), pendingOAuthTransports.clear())

Status_types.code: export const Status = Schema.Union([Schema.Literal("connected"), Schema.Literal("connecting"), Schema.Literal("failed"), Schema.Literal("disabled"), Schema.Literal("needs_auth"), Schema.Literal("needs_client_registration"), Schema.Literal("needs_oauth_redirect")])

error_handling.create_silent_failure: MCP init wraps create() in Effect.catch(() => Effect.void) — all connection errors are silently suppressed; only disabled/connected statuses are recorded
error_handling.transport_connection_failure: connectTransport catches errors via Effect.tryPromise; on failure, transport.close() is called and error is mapped to { status: "failed", error: ... }
error_handling.invalid_config_skipped: Config entries without 'type' field are logged as error (line 576: log.error("Ignoring MCP config entry without type", { key })) and skipped
error_handling.disabled_servers: Servers with enabled: false show status "disabled" and are not connected
error_handling.child_process_cleanup: On scope close, all MCP child processes are killed via SIGTERM with try/catch (line 610); client.close() wrapped in try/catch (line 613)
error_handling.semaphore_lock_timeout: 30-second timeout on per-server semaphore locks; returns { status: "failed", error: "Semaphore lock timed out" }

init_phases.description: Native/binary dependency loading at startup — on-demand spawns, not static loading

subsystems.name: LLM Native Runtime (@opencode-ai/llm)
subsystems.binding_type: TypeScript npm package (no native addon, no FFI, no .node file)
subsystems.loading_trigger: On-demand during LLM streaming — caller checks status() first to determine if native path is supported

status_check.code: export function status(input): RuntimeStatus — checks providerID is openai/anthropic/opencode, npm package is @ai-sdk/openai/openai-compatible/anthropic, API key configured

stream_execution.code: export function stream(input): StreamResult — constructs LLMNative.request() and passes to input.llmClient.stream()

provider_configuration.code: export const model = (input, headers?) — maps ai-sdk npm packages to native provider configurations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Google, AmazonBedrock, OpenAICompatible, OpenRouter)

request_construction.code: export const request = (input) => LLM.request({ model: model(input), system, messages, tools, ... })

subsystems.error_handling: status() returns { type: "unsupported", reason: ... } when provider/model not supported; stream falls through to AI SDK path when unsupported. Tool execution errors wrapped in ToolFailure.

subsystems.name: DuckDB Analytics
subsystems.loading_trigger: On-demand for DDL/INSERT operations — NOT loaded at startup

execDuckDB.code: export function execDuckDB(dbPath, sql, signal): Promise<void> — spawn duckdb with -json -c flags; 60s timeout

execDuckDBStdin.code: export function execDuckDBStdin(dbPath, sql, data, signal): Promise<void> — spawn duckdb with stdin JSON pipe

subsystems.error_handling: Process failures reject with DuckDBWriteError; 60s timeout; AbortSignal support via signal listener

subsystems.name: Ripgrep (rg)
subsystems.binding_type: Effect ChildProcessSpawner via CrossSpawnSpawner — auto-downloads binary
subsystems.loading_trigger: On-demand for files/tree/search operations — NOT loaded at startup

layer_definition.code: export const layer = Layer.effect(Service, ...) — provides files(), tree(), search() functions

platform_detection.code: const PLATFORM = { "arm64-darwin": { platform: "aarch64-apple-darwin", extension: "tar.gz" }, ... } — auto-downloads correct rg binary

subsystems.error_handling: Process errors produce typed errors (not crash); abort signal support

error_handling.all_native_deps: All native/binary dependencies are loaded on-demand (lazy spawn), not at startup. No static dlopen/FFI/WASM/.node imports found. Startup is not blocked by missing binaries.

layer_composition_order.description: Effect Layer mergeAll ordering in AppLayer — determines service initialization order
layer_composition_order.code: export const AppLayer: Layer.Layer<any, never, never> = Layer.mergeAll(Npm.defaultLayer, AppFileSystem.defaultLayer, Bus.defaultLayer, Auth.defaultLayer, Account.defaultLayer, Config.defaultLayer, Git.defaultLayer, Ripgrep.defaultLayer, File.defaultLayer, FileWatcher.defaultLayer, Storage.defaultLayer, Snapshot.defaultLayer, Plugin.defaultLayer, ModelsDev.defaultLayer, Provider.defaultLayer, ProviderAuth.defaultLayer, Agent.defaultLayer, Skill.defaultLayer, Discovery.defaultLayer, Question.defaultLayer, Permission.defaultLayer, Todo.defaultLayer, Session.defaultLayer, SessionStatus.defaultLayer, BackgroundJob.defaultLayer, RuntimeFlags.defaultLayer, DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer, SessionRunState.defaultLayer, SessionProcessor.defaultLayer, SessionCompaction.defaultLayer, SessionRevert.defaultLayer, SessionSummary.defaultLayer, SessionPrompt.defaultLayer, Instruction.defaultLayer, LLM.defaultLayer, LSP.defaultLayer, MCP.defaultLayer, McpAuth.defaultLayer, Command.defaultLayer, Truncate.defaultLayer, ToolCache.defaultLayer, ToolRegistry.defaultLayer, Format.defaultLayer, Project.defaultLayer, Vcs.defaultLayer, Reference.defaultLayer, Workspace.defaultLayer, Worktree.appLayer, Pty.defaultLayer, PtyTicket.defaultLayer, Installation.defaultLayer, ShareNext.defaultLayer, SessionShare.defaultLayer, SyncEvent.defaultLayer, EventV2Bridge.defaultLayer, DataMigration.defaultLayer).pipe(Layer.provideMerge(DatabaseConfig.defaultLayer), Layer.provideMerge(InstanceLayer.layer), Layer.provideMerge(Observability.layer), Layer.provideMerge(GitHubService.defaultLayer))
notes: Plugin (position 13) initializes early — after core infrastructure (Npm, Bus, Auth, Config, Git, File, Storage) but before Provider, Session, LLM
MCP (position 37) initializes late — after Session, LLM, LSP, alongside McpAuth
BootstrapRuntime (bootstrap-runtime.ts:17) also includes Plugin.defaultLayer but NOT MCP.defaultLayer — MCP is only available in full AppRuntime
Plugin.init() is explicitly called first during instance bootstrap (bootstrap.ts:45) with comment 'Plugin can mutate config so it has to be initialized before anything else.'

bootstrap_execution_order.description: Per-instance bootstrap runs plugin init first, then all other services concurrently
code: 1. config.get() — eager load config for traces (line 44)
2. plugin.init() — MUST run first; can mutate config (line 46)
3. Effect.forEach([reference, lsp, shareNext, format, file, fileWatcher, vcs, snapshot, project], init) — concurrent, each wrapped in Effect.catchCause (lines 49-53)
bootstrap_execution_order.error_handling: Each service init is wrapped in Effect.catchCause(() => Effect.logWarning("init failed", { cause })). No single service failure crashes boot.

init_projectors.code: import "./init-projectors" — side-effect import that calls initProjectors() to register Sync event projectors

listen_function.code: export async function listen(opts: ListenOptions): Promise<Listener> — starts HTTP server, sets up mDNS, returns listener with stop()

listenerLayer.code: function listenerLayer(opts, port) — builds HttpRouter.serve with disposeMiddleware, WebSocketTracker, serverLayer, ConfigProvider

Default_export.code: export const Default = lazy(() => { const handler = HttpApiApp.webHandler().handler; return { app: { fetch, request } } })

cli_entry.code: export async function bootstrap<T>(directory: string, cb: () => Promise<T>) { const ctx = await InstanceRuntime.load({ directory }); try { return await context.provide(ctx, cb) } finally { await InstanceRuntime.disposeInstance(ctx) } }
cli_entry.notes: InstanceRuntime.load() goes through InstanceStore.load() → boot() → bootstrap.run() which triggers plugin.init() → parallel service inits
